questions.learningObjective: Verify AIRAC cycle currency before a navigation database load.
questions.fullObjective: Given an FMS navigation database update, identify the first verification the technician must perform before loading the database.
questions.targetSourceStem: Before loading a navigation database update into an FMS, what must the technician verify first?

flashcard.front: What must the technician verify first before loading a navigation database update into an FMS?
flashcard.back: That the database effective date matches the current AIRAC cycle for the intended service window.
flashcard.explanation: AIRAC effectivity is the threshold check. Flight recency, autopilot self-test, and display power-state are not prerequisites for a nav-database load.

jeopardy.clue: This is the first thing a technician verifies before loading an FMS navigation database update.
choices: Aircraft has flown within the last 30 days
All cockpit displays are currently powered on
Autopilot servo self-test has passed
Database effective date matches current AIRAC cycle
jeopardy.explanation: The effective date must match the current AIRAC cycle and cover the intended period of use.

multipleChoice.stem: Before loading an FMS navigation database update, what must the technician verify first?
choices: Every cockpit display is already powered on
The aircraft has flown within the last 30 days
The autopilot servo self-test has just passed
The database effective date matches the current AIRAC cycle
multipleChoice.explanation: AIRAC cycle currency is the first prerequisite for a nav-database load.

practice.stem: A technician is about to load a nav database. What is the first verification?
choices: All displays power up
Aircraft flew within 30 days
Autopilot servos self-test pass
AIRAC cycle is current
practice.explanation: Verify the database effective date matches the current AIRAC cycle before dispatch; a stale database fails IFR legality.

preassessment.stem: First verification before an FMS nav-database load?
choices: Displays powered
Recent flight activity
Autopilot self-test
AIRAC effective date match

questions.learningObjective: Recover from an avionics software load that fails at 80%.
questions.fullObjective: Given an avionics software load that fails at 80 percent and displays INVALID, identify the correct recovery action.
questions.targetSourceStem: A technician is updating avionics software using a data loader. The upload fails at 80% and the unit displays 'INVALID'. What is the correct recovery action?

flashcard.front: A software load fails at 80% with an INVALID message. What is the correct recovery action?
flashcard.back: Cycle the unit's power and attempt the load again per the maintenance manual.
flashcard.explanation: Power-cycle and retry is the documented recovery. Unit return, rollback, and flying with invalid software are not acceptable initial actions.

jeopardy.clue: This is the correct action after a software load fails at 80% with INVALID displayed.
choices: Cycle power and attempt the load again
Return the unit to the manufacturer
Install the previous software version
Continue flight operations and retry later
jeopardy.explanation: Power-cycle and retry is the first recovery step per most avionics maintenance manuals.

multipleChoice.stem: During a software update the loader fails at 80% and the unit shows INVALID. What is the correct recovery action?
choices: Power cycle the unit and attempt the load again
Return the unit directly to the manufacturer
Roll back to the previous software version immediately
Release the aircraft and retry during next flight
multipleChoice.explanation: Cycle power and retry the load.

practice.stem: Software load fails at 80%; display reads INVALID. What should the technician do next?
choices: Cycle power and attempt the load again
Ship the unit to the manufacturer
Install the previous software version
Continue flight operations and retry later
practice.explanation: Power-cycle and retry is the first step.

preassessment.stem: Recovery for software load fail at 80% INVALID?
choices: Cycle power and retry
Return to manufacturer
Rollback software
Fly and retry later

questions.learningObjective: Save configuration before an integrated-avionics software load.
questions.fullObjective: Given an integrated avionics software load, identify the step taken before loading to prevent data loss.
questions.targetSourceStem: Before performing any software load on integrated avionics, what step should the technician always perform to prevent data loss?

flashcard.front: What pre-load step prevents data loss during integrated avionics software updates?
flashcard.back: Export and save the current configuration to an external medium before starting the load.
flashcard.explanation: Saving configuration protects pilot-entered preferences and aircraft-specific data if the load resets them. Battery disconnect and brightness-note are not appropriate steps.

jeopardy.clue: This action should always precede a software load on integrated avionics to prevent data loss.
choices: Reset all systems to factory defaults
Export and save current configuration
Disconnect the aircraft battery
Document the current display brightness settings
jeopardy.explanation: Saving the configuration protects aircraft-specific data in case the update clears settings.

multipleChoice.stem: Before any software load on integrated avionics, which pre-load step prevents data loss?
choices: Reset the system to factory defaults
Export and save the current configuration
Disconnect the aircraft battery
Record the current brightness settings
multipleChoice.explanation: Saving the configuration allows restoration if the load resets it.

practice.stem: A technician is about to load software on integrated avionics. What should be done first to prevent data loss?
choices: Reset unit to factory defaults
Export and save configuration
Disconnect the aircraft battery
Record display brightness setting
practice.explanation: Export and save the current configuration before loading new software so settings can be restored if needed.

preassessment.stem: Pre-load step that prevents data loss on integrated avionics?
choices: Record brightness setting
Export configuration file
Perform factory reset
Disconnect the battery
preassessment.explanation: Exporting and saving the current configuration preserves settings before any load or software update.

questions.learningObjective: Locate the compatible hardware configuration part number for FMS software.
questions.fullObjective: Given an FMS software load requiring a compatible hardware configuration part number, identify where that part number is documented.
questions.targetSourceStem: An FMS software load requires 'compatible hardware configuration part number'. Where is this information found?

flashcard.front: Where is the FMS 'compatible hardware configuration part number' documented?
flashcard.back: In the aircraft equipment list or on the FMS data plate.
flashcard.explanation: Compatibility is aircraft-specific and lives on the equipment list or the LRU data plate. The operating manual, database header, and POH limitations do not carry this configuration number.

jeopardy.clue: This is where the FMS 'compatible hardware configuration part number' is documented.
choices: Navigation database header file
FMS operating manual table of contents
Aircraft equipment list or FMS data plate
Pilot operating handbook limitations
jeopardy.explanation: The compatible hardware part number lives on the aircraft equipment list or the LRU data plate.

multipleChoice.stem: An FMS software load asks for the compatible hardware configuration part number. Where does the technician find it?
choices: In the navigation database header file
In the FMS operating manual table of contents
On the aircraft equipment list or the FMS data plate
In the pilot operating handbook limitations
multipleChoice.explanation: Equipment list or data plate is the authoritative source.

practice.stem: Before loading FMS software, the technician needs the compatible hardware configuration part number. Where is it located?
choices: The navigation database header file
The FMS operating manual table of contents
The aircraft equipment list or the FMS data plate
The pilot operating handbook limitations
practice.explanation: Check the equipment list or data plate.

preassessment.stem: Where is the FMS compatible hardware config PN?
choices: Nav database header page
Operating manual TOC
Equipment list or data plate
POH limitations section
preassessment.explanation: Installed equipment is documented on the aircraft equipment list or data plate.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the verification required before returning a display unit to service after a software load.
questions.fullObjective: Given a completed display-unit software load, identify the verification required before the aircraft returns to service.
questions.targetSourceStem: After loading updated software on a display unit, what verification is required before returning to service?

flashcard.front: What verification must be completed before returning a display unit to service after a software update?
flashcard.back: An operational test performed in accordance with the maintenance manual procedures.
flashcard.explanation: The operational test in the maintenance manual is the required post-load verification. Flight test, manufacturer certification, and FSDO approval are not standard RTS steps.

jeopardy.clue: This verification is required before returning a display unit to service after a software update.
choices: Manufacturer certification of the software version
Flight test verifying every display function
FSDO inspector approval of the installation
Operational test per maintenance manual procedures
jeopardy.explanation: An operational test per the maintenance manual is the required post-load verification.

multipleChoice.stem: Following a display unit software load, which verification is required before returning to service?
choices: Manufacturer certification of the software version
A flight test to verify all display functions
FSDO inspector approval of the installation
An operational test per the maintenance manual procedures
multipleChoice.explanation: The maintenance manual operational test is the required post-load verification.

practice.stem: A display unit has just been reloaded with updated software. What verification is required before RTS?
choices: FSDO inspector approval of the installation
Flight test verifying every display function
Manufacturer certification of the software version
Operational test per maintenance manual procedures
practice.explanation: An operational test per the maintenance manual is required before RTS.

preassessment.stem: Post-software-load verification for a display unit?
choices: FSDO field approval
Flight test of all functions
Manufacturer certification
Operational test per manual
preassessment.explanation: An operational test per the maintenance manual is the standard acceptance step for returning installed avionics to service.
